description: Maps DeepInspect's 14 visual defect categories to CODEBRIM's 6 multi-label classes (CVPR 2019)
target_paper: Mundt et al. (2019) CVPR — CODEBRIM: Concrete Defect Bridge Image Segmentation Dataset
notes: CODEBRIM uses multi-label image classification (not pixel segmentation like dacl10k).
One image can have multiple CODEBRIM classes simultaneously.
CODEBRIM covers concrete bridges only — steel and timber defects are out of scope.
DeepInspect categories with no CODEBRIM equivalent must rely on other evidence sources.
CODEBRIM training accuracy: ~85% mAP on held-out test set (original paper).

codebrim_classes.name: crack
codebrim_classes.description: Crack in concrete — linear fracture of any width or orientation

codebrim_classes.name: spalling
codebrim_classes.description: Concrete cover loss — detachment of surface layer revealing aggregate

codebrim_classes.name: efflorescence
codebrim_classes.description: White salt crystal deposits on concrete surface from water migration

codebrim_classes.name: exposed_reinforcement
codebrim_classes.description: Reinforcing steel bars visible through cover loss, with or without active corrosion

codebrim_classes.name: corrosion_stain
codebrim_classes.description: Rust/iron oxide staining on concrete surface from corroding embedded steel

codebrim_classes.name: bearing_failure
codebrim_classes.description: Visible deterioration or failure of bridge bearings
codebrim_classes.detection_accuracy: medium (68% precision — small objects, often occluded)

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: Direct 1:1 correspondence. CODEBRIM crack class covers all crack types visible in inspection photos.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.limitations: CODEBRIM does not distinguish crack type (structural vs. shrinkage vs. fatigue). Crack severity must be assessed from image context, not class label alone.
CODEBRIM spalling class does not quantify area. Extent assessment requires additional analysis.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: Direct 1:1 correspondence. Presence of CODEBRIM class 3 automatically sets DeepInspect score floor at 4.0 for affected criterion.
Primary: corrosion_stain (class 4) indicates active rust. Secondary: exposed_reinforcement (class 3) when rebar is visibly corroded. Both together indicate severe corrosion state.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.scoring_logic: class 4 alone → score 3.0-4.0; class 3 + class 4 → score 4.0-5.0

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: Direct 1:1 correspondence. Efflorescence indicates water migration pathway — not a structural defect itself but a durability indicator.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.score_cap_note: Efflorescence alone should not exceed 3.5 without co-occurring cracks or spalling.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: Surface degradation uses spalling (class 1) and efflorescence (class 2) as evidence proxies. Neither alone constitutes the full category.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.limitations: CODEBRIM does not have a general surface degradation class. Carbonation, weathering, and chemical attack are not distinguished.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: Direct 1:1 correspondence via CODEBRIM bearing_failure class.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.limitations: CODEBRIM bearing_failure detection accuracy is lowest of all 6 classes (68%). Bearings are small objects often partially occluded. Supplement with manual review.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: No CODEBRIM class covers structural deformation or geometric anomalies. CODEBRIM is entirely surface-defect focused. Deformation must be assessed independently.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.alternative: Use geometric analysis of Street View imagery or photogrammetry. CODEBRIM provides no evidence.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: No CODEBRIM class for drainage defects. CODEBRIM dataset does not include drainage elements.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.alternative: Assess from Street View geometric context (standing water, staining patterns below drain positions).

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: No CODEBRIM class for expansion joints. CODEBRIM dataset does not include joint close-ups.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.alternative: Use dacl10k expansion_joint_deterioration class or direct visual assessment.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: No CODEBRIM class for railings or safety equipment. CODEBRIM is limited to concrete structural element defects.
Corrosion stain (class 4) provides partial evidence for protective coating failure — rust bleeding indicates coating has failed in the affected area.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.limitations: Does not cover waterproofing or cathodic protection systems.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: Pier condition is assessed from the combination of defect classes detected on pier elements. Any of crack, spalling, exposed_reinforcement, or corrosion_stain on piers contributes to pier condition score.
deepinspect_to_codebrim.scoring_logic: Highest severity class detected on pier determines primary score. Co-occurrence of multiple classes increases score.

deepinspect_to_codebrim.notes: Same logic as pier_condition but applied to abutment elements. Efflorescence is more commonly relevant for abutments (soil-side water migration).

combined_severity_matrix.description: When multiple CODEBRIM classes co-occur, use this matrix to determine composite severity

combinations.interpretation: Exposed reinforcement + corrosion stain → active rebar corrosion
Spalling with exposed reinforcement → critical cover loss
Crack + exposed rebar + rust → imminent structural risk
Efflorescence only → durability concern, not immediate structural risk
